fields.Text:
Prior sacral chordoma resection with new multifocal epidural lumbar soft tissue masses. Most likely finding?

{{c1::Epidural metastatic/recurrent chordoma}}
fields.Extra: Q1. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Epidural recurrence of resected sacral chordoma is typically multifocal involving the sacral canal with extension into mesorectal space, gluteal muscles, and residual sacrum. Magnetic resonance imaging demonstrates soft tissue masses requiring wide margins for salvage radiotherapy planning due to complex recurrence patterns.

fields.Text: Sacral chordoma recurrence can be {{c1::multifocal epidural disease}} with extension along the sacral canal and spine.
fields.Extra: Q1. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Epidural recurrence of resected sacral chordoma is typically multifocal involving the sacral canal with extension into mesorectal space, gluteal muscles, and residual sacrum. Magnetic resonance imaging demonstrates soft tissue masses requiring wide margins for salvage radiotherapy planning due to complex recurrence patterns.

fields.Text: Increasing scintillator {{c1::light decay time}} decreases PET detector efficiency by increasing dead time at high count rates.
fields.Extra: Q2. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Increased scintillator light decay time causes detector paralysis at high count rates because the detector remains "busy" longer after each event, creating dead time during which new photons cannot be registered. This reduces effective detection efficiency and count rate capability, limiting overall system performance.

fields.Text: Increasing PET detector thickness, density, or atomic number improves photon absorption, whereas increased {{c1::light decay time}} worsens detector efficiency.
fields.Extra: Q2. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Increased scintillator light decay time causes detector paralysis at high count rates because the detector remains "busy" longer after each event, creating dead time during which new photons cannot be registered. This reduces effective detection efficiency and count rate capability, limiting overall system performance.

fields.Text:
Small echogenic kidneys with loss of corticomedullary differentiation bilaterally. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Chronic medical renal disease}}
fields.Extra: Q3. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Small echogenic kidneys indicate chronic kidney disease with irreversible parenchymal loss, distinguishing chronic from acute kidney injury where kidneys are typically normal-sized or enlarged. This pattern represents end-stage changes from longstanding medical renal disease.

fields.Text: Small echogenic kidneys with loss of corticomedullary differentiation indicate {{c1::chronic medical renal disease}}.
fields.Extra: Q3. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Small echogenic kidneys indicate chronic kidney disease with irreversible parenchymal loss, distinguishing chronic from acute kidney injury where kidneys are typically normal-sized or enlarged. This pattern represents end-stage changes from longstanding medical renal disease.

fields.Text:
Large pulmonary embolism with RV enlargement compared with LV. What prognostic CT finding is shown?

{{c1::Right ventricular strain}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q4. Correct; difficulty: moderate. The CT study shows a large saddle pulmonary embolism (red arrows).

fields.Text: In acute pulmonary embolism, an RV:LV ratio greater than 1 indicates {{c1::right ventricular strain}} and predicts worse prognosis.
fields.Extra: Q4. Correct; difficulty: moderate. The CT study shows a large saddle pulmonary embolism (red arrows).

fields.Text: Asthma {{c1::does increase}} the likelihood of an allergic-like iodinated contrast reaction, especially bronchospasm.
fields.Extra: Q5. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: The approach to patients about to undergo a contrast-enhanced examination has four general goals:

fields.Text:
Aortic arch anomaly with retroesophageal right subclavian artery origin. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Aberrant right subclavian artery with Kommerell diverticulum}}

1/5
fields.Extra: Q6. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Left-sided aortic arch with aberrant right subclavian artery is the most common congenital anomaly of the aortic arch. Associated with Kommerell's diverticulum and vascular ring. Most patients with this entity are asymptomatic, though some can present with dysphagia lusoria.

fields.Text: A left aortic arch with aberrant right subclavian artery is associated with {{c1::Kommerell diverticulum}} at the aberrant vessel origin.
fields.Extra: Q6. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Left-sided aortic arch with aberrant right subclavian artery is the most common congenital anomaly of the aortic arch. Associated with Kommerell's diverticulum and vascular ring. Most patients with this entity are asymptomatic, though some can present with dysphagia lusoria.

fields.Text:
PET image with apparent diffuse uptake in lung and subcutaneous fat. Most likely explanation?

{{c1::Non-attenuation-corrected PET image}}
fields.Extra: Q7. Correct; difficulty: easy. Any time there is diffuse "uptake" in non-dense tissue such as lungs or the subcutaneous fat (as seen here), suspicion for non-attenuation correction should arise.

fields.Text: Diffuse apparent uptake in non-dense tissues on PET should raise suspicion for a {{c1::non-attenuation-corrected image}}.
fields.Extra: Q7. Correct; difficulty: easy. Any time there is diffuse "uptake" in non-dense tissue such as lungs or the subcutaneous fat (as seen here), suspicion for non-attenuation correction should arise.

fields.Text:
Foot radiographs with lateral displacement of all metatarsals at the TMT joints. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Homolateral Lisfranc fracture-dislocation}}

1/3
fields.Extra: Q8. Correct; difficulty: hard. In homolateral Lisfranc, there is lateral displacement of the 1st to 5th metatarsals, or lateral dislocation of the 2nd to 5th metatarsals while the 1st tarsometatarsal (TMT) joint remains congruent with the first or medial cuneiform.

fields.Text: Homolateral Lisfranc injury produces {{c1::lateral displacement of all metatarsals}} at the tarsometatarsal joints.
fields.Extra: Q8. Correct; difficulty: hard. In homolateral Lisfranc, there is lateral displacement of the 1st to 5th metatarsals, or lateral dislocation of the 2nd to 5th metatarsals while the 1st tarsometatarsal (TMT) joint remains congruent with the first or medial cuneiform.

fields.Text:
55-year-old man with ulnar-sided wrist pain and disruption of the articular disc attachments. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Triangular fibrocartilage complex tear}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q9. Incorrect; difficulty: easy. The coronal proton density and gradient echo sequences through the wrist show complete disruption of the fovea (red arrows) and styloid (yellow arrows) portions of articular disc of the TFCC, indicative of a complex tear of the TFCC.

fields.Text: The TFCC stabilizes the {{c1::distal radioulnar joint}} and commonly presents with ulnar-sided wrist pain when torn.
fields.Extra: Q9. Incorrect; difficulty: easy. The coronal proton density and gradient echo sequences through the wrist show complete disruption of the fovea (red arrows) and styloid (yellow arrows) portions of articular disc of the TFCC, indicative of a complex tear of the TFCC.

fields.Text: Ulnar-sided wrist pain with articular disc disruption at the foveal/styloid attachments is a {{c1::TFCC tear}}, not a simple lunotriquetral ligament tear.
fields.Extra: Q9. Incorrect; difficulty: easy. The coronal proton density and gradient echo sequences through the wrist show complete disruption of the fovea (red arrows) and styloid (yellow arrows) portions of articular disc of the TFCC, indicative of a complex tear of the TFCC.

fields.Text:
Ultrasound image with artificial posterior displacement of the liver/diaphragm interface. What artifact is shown?

{{c1::Speed displacement artifact}}
fields.Extra: Q10. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: The apparent diaphragmatic discontinuity represents boundary distortion from speed displacement—fatty infiltration slows ultrasound transit, causing time-based depth miscalculation that makes the diaphragm appear artificially deeper in fat-affected areas, creating false discontinuity of an actually smooth interface.

fields.Text: Speed displacement artifact occurs when sound travels slower than assumed, causing structures to be displayed {{c1::deeper than their true position}}.
fields.Extra: Q10. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: The apparent diaphragmatic discontinuity represents boundary distortion from speed displacement—fatty infiltration slows ultrasound transit, causing time-based depth miscalculation that makes the diaphragm appear artificially deeper in fat-affected areas, creating false discontinuity of an actually smooth interface.

fields.Text:
Whole-body thyroid cancer scan with intense thyroid-bed uptake and star artifact. Most likely radiotracer?

{{c1::I-131 sodium iodide}}
fields.Extra: Q11. Correct; difficulty: hard. This whole-body thyroid cancer scan was obtained seven days after high-dose I-131 therapy.

fields.Text: I-131 imaging can produce a {{c1::star artifact}} from septal penetration by high-energy photons.
fields.Extra: Q11. Correct; difficulty: hard. This whole-body thyroid cancer scan was obtained seven days after high-dose I-131 therapy.

fields.Text:
Planimetry of the aortic valve shows stenosis. Most common cause in the United States?

{{c1::Age-associated degenerative calcific disease}}
fields.Extra: Q12. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Degenerative calcific disease is the most common cause of aortic stenosis in developed countries. Rheumatic disease is the leading cause in developing countries.

fields.Text: In developed countries, the most common cause of aortic stenosis is {{c1::age-associated degenerative calcific disease}}.
fields.Extra: Q12. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Degenerative calcific disease is the most common cause of aortic stenosis in developed countries. Rheumatic disease is the leading cause in developing countries.

fields.Text: Rheumatic disease is the leading cause of aortic stenosis in developing countries, but in the United States the most common cause is {{c1::degenerative calcific disease}}.
fields.Extra: Q12. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Degenerative calcific disease is the most common cause of aortic stenosis in developed countries. Rheumatic disease is the leading cause in developing countries.

fields.Text:
Cardiac MRI with an encapsulated right atrial mass that follows fat signal. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Right atrial lipoma}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q13. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Cardiac lipoma on imaging typically presents as an encapsulated mass of adipose tissue that follows fat signal on all sequences, most commonly at the atrial septum, right atrium, and left ventricle.

fields.Text: Cardiac lipoma follows {{c1::fat signal on all MRI sequences}} and may occur in the atrial septum, right atrium, or left ventricle.
fields.Extra: Q13. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Cardiac lipoma on imaging typically presents as an encapsulated mass of adipose tissue that follows fat signal on all sequences, most commonly at the atrial septum, right atrium, and left ventricle.

fields.Text: A cardiac mass that suppresses like fat on fat-saturated MRI is a {{c1::lipoma}}, not a thrombus or myxoma.
fields.Extra: Q13. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Cardiac lipoma on imaging typically presents as an encapsulated mass of adipose tissue that follows fat signal on all sequences, most commonly at the atrial septum, right atrium, and left ventricle.

fields.Text:
Carotid Doppler waveform with delayed upstroke and low amplitude. Most likely associated diagnosis?

{{c1::Aortic stenosis}}
fields.Extra: Q14. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Severe aortic stenosis produces characteristic carotid duplex findings of delayed systolic upstroke with blunted amplitude and rounded waveform morphology, termed "pulsus tardus et parvus," while peak systolic velocities remain relatively preserved unless concurrent left ventricular dysfunction is present.

fields.Text: Pulsus tardus et parvus on carotid Doppler suggests {{c1::proximal aortic stenosis}}.
fields.Extra: Q14. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Severe aortic stenosis produces characteristic carotid duplex findings of delayed systolic upstroke with blunted amplitude and rounded waveform morphology, termed "pulsus tardus et parvus," while peak systolic velocities remain relatively preserved unless concurrent left ventricular dysfunction is present.

fields.Text:
Six-week postpartum patient with a breast lump and avascular echogenic/cystic milk-containing lesion. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Galactocele}}
fields.Extra: Q15. Incorrect; difficulty: moderate. This is a galactocele.

fields.Text: A postpartum milk-containing breast mass without internal Doppler flow is most consistent with a {{c1::galactocele}}.
fields.Extra: Q15. Incorrect; difficulty: moderate. This is a galactocele.

fields.Text: Postpartum galactocele can appear solid and echogenic on ultrasound when {{c1::milk fat is in suspension}}.
fields.Extra: Q15. Incorrect; difficulty: moderate. This is a galactocele.

fields.Text:
MRI spin-echo sequence diagram. What is the purpose of the 180-degree refocusing pulse?

{{c1::Minimizes T2* artifact}}
fields.Extra: Q16. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: The 180° pulse is essential because it reverses dephasing from magnetic field inhomogeneities while preserving true T2 information, enabling the echo formation necessary for spin echo imaging and accurate tissue characterization.

fields.Text: A spin-echo 180-degree refocusing pulse minimizes {{c1::T2* effects from magnetic field inhomogeneity}}.
fields.Extra: Q16. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: The 180° pulse is essential because it reverses dephasing from magnetic field inhomogeneities while preserving true T2 information, enabling the echo formation necessary for spin echo imaging and accurate tissue characterization.

fields.Text:
2-year-old boy with a large multicystic renal mass. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Pediatric cystic nephroma}}
fields.Extra: Q17. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Multilocular cystic nephroma is a cystic renal mass with typical appearance of a large multicystic mass with variably enhancing internal septations. On ultrasounds, cyst contents are usually anechoic, but low-level echoes may be seen, and septal vascularity can also be seen.

fields.Text: Pediatric cystic nephroma is a large {{c1::multicystic renal mass with enhancing septations}} and is most common from infancy to toddler age.
fields.Extra: Q17. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Multilocular cystic nephroma is a cystic renal mass with typical appearance of a large multicystic mass with variably enhancing internal septations. On ultrasounds, cyst contents are usually anechoic, but low-level echoes may be seen, and septal vascularity can also be seen.

fields.Text: CME and maintenance of certification reflect commitment to {{c1::professional competence}}.
fields.Extra: Q18. Correct; difficulty: moderate. The medical profession adheres to fundamental principles and the fulfillment of definitive professional responsibilities.

fields.Text:
Bone scan with left collecting-system tracer retention in a patient with back pain. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Obstructing nephrolithiasis}}
fields.Extra: Q19. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Obstructive hydronephrosis on bone scintigraphy can manifest as either diffusely increased uptake (due to tracer retention from impaired drainage) or diffusely decreased uptake (from severely compromised renal function), requiring correlation with anatomic imaging for definitive diagnosis.

fields.Text: On bone scintigraphy, obstructive hydronephrosis can manifest as {{c1::retained tracer activity in the collecting system}}.
fields.Extra: Q19. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Obstructive hydronephrosis on bone scintigraphy can manifest as either diffusely increased uptake (due to tracer retention from impaired drainage) or diffusely decreased uptake (from severely compromised renal function), requiring correlation with anatomic imaging for definitive diagnosis.

fields.Text:
Axial head CT with CSF space posterior to the forniceal columns. What normal variant is shown?

{{c1::Cavum vergae}}
fields.Extra: Q20. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Cavum vergae is the cerebrospinal fluid space posterior to the forniceal columns that normally closes before birth. When persistent, it's usually seen with cavum septum pellucidum and is considered a normal variant. It must be distinguished from cavum velum interpositum, which lies below the fornix rather than posterior to it.

fields.Text: Cavum vergae is the CSF space {{c1::posterior to the forniceal columns}} and is usually a normal variant.
fields.Extra: Q20. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Cavum vergae is the cerebrospinal fluid space posterior to the forniceal columns that normally closes before birth. When persistent, it's usually seen with cavum septum pellucidum and is considered a normal variant. It must be distinguished from cavum velum interpositum, which lies below the fornix rather than posterior to it.

fields.Text: Pulmonary nodule features associated with malignancy include {{c1::irregular or spiculated margins}}.
fields.Extra: Q21. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Characteristics of a pulmonary nodule associated with a greater likelihood of malignancy include spiculated or irregular margins, semi-solid composition, slow doubling time, and an absence of calcifications.

fields.Text:
Young woman with hemorrhagic heterogeneous enhancing liver mass. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Hepatic adenoma}}
fields.Extra: Q22. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Hepatocellular adenomas appear heterogeneous on CT due to intrinsic components including microscopic fat, hemorrhage or blood products, and structural features that vary by molecular subtype.

fields.Text: Hepatic adenoma is favored in a young woman with a heterogeneous liver mass containing {{c1::fat, hemorrhage, or blood products}}.
fields.Extra: Q22. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Hepatocellular adenomas appear heterogeneous on CT due to intrinsic components including microscopic fat, hemorrhage or blood products, and structural features that vary by molecular subtype.

fields.Text: In mammography, lower dose is associated with {{c1::less dense and thinner breasts}} because less mAs is required.
fields.Extra: Q23. Correct; difficulty: easy. Absorbed dose in mammography is a function of many factors, two of which are breast density and breast thickness.

fields.Text:
Bone PET performed for breast cancer staging. Most likely radiotracer?

{{c1::F-18 sodium fluoride}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q24. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Metastases to bone are a common source of malignancy in the skeleton, and they occur much more frequently than primary bone tumors.

fields.Text: F-18 NaF is a PET bone agent used for detecting {{c1::osseous metastases}}.
fields.Extra: Q24. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Metastases to bone are a common source of malignancy in the skeleton, and they occur much more frequently than primary bone tumors.

fields.Text: A PET bone scan for metastatic disease uses {{c1::F-18 NaF}}, whereas Tc-99m MDP is used for conventional planar/SPECT bone scintigraphy.
fields.Extra: Q24. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Metastases to bone are a common source of malignancy in the skeleton, and they occur much more frequently than primary bone tumors.

fields.Text:
Renal scintigraphy with early gallbladder activity after tracer injection. What tracer-clearance mechanism is tested?

{{c1::Tubular secretion}}
fields.Extra: Q25. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: MAG3 is cleared by proximal tubular secretion (95%) for measuring effective renal plasma flow, while DTPA is cleared by glomerular filtration for measuring GFR. MAG3 is superior in decreased kidney function because tubular secretion is often preserved when glomerular filtration is impaired.

fields.Text: Tc-99m MAG3 is cleared predominantly by {{c1::proximal tubular secretion}}.
fields.Extra: Q25. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: MAG3 is cleared by proximal tubular secretion (95%) for measuring effective renal plasma flow, while DTPA is cleared by glomerular filtration for measuring GFR. MAG3 is superior in decreased kidney function because tubular secretion is often preserved when glomerular filtration is impaired.

fields.Text:
17-year-old with low-lying cerebellar tonsils and cervical syrinx. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Chiari 1 malformation}}
fields.Extra: Q26. Incorrect; difficulty: not listed. Sagittal T1-weighted magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) shown demonstrates low-lying cerebellar tonsils extending 1 to 2 cm below foramen magnum (arrow) and cystic intramedullary structure central in the cervicothoracic spinal cord.

fields.Text: Chiari 1 malformation is characterized by {{c1::low-lying cerebellar tonsils}} and can be associated with a syrinx.
fields.Extra: Q26. Incorrect; difficulty: not listed. Sagittal T1-weighted magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) shown demonstrates low-lying cerebellar tonsils extending 1 to 2 cm below foramen magnum (arrow) and cystic intramedullary structure central in the cervicothoracic spinal cord.

fields.Text: Low-lying cerebellar tonsils with a syrinx indicate {{c1::Chiari 1 malformation}}, not isolated syringomyelia as the primary diagnosis.
fields.Extra: Q26. Incorrect; difficulty: not listed. Sagittal T1-weighted magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) shown demonstrates low-lying cerebellar tonsils extending 1 to 2 cm below foramen magnum (arrow) and cystic intramedullary structure central in the cervicothoracic spinal cord.

fields.Text:
Jugular foramen mass with broad dural tail and nonaggressive bony remodeling. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Meningioma}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q27. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. The images provided show a mass (red arrow) within the jugular foramen with a broad-based dural tail (green arrow) and non-aggressive bony remodeling (yellow arrow).

fields.Text: A jugular foramen mass with a broad dural tail and nonaggressive bony remodeling favors {{c1::meningioma}}.
fields.Extra: Q27. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. The images provided show a mass (red arrow) within the jugular foramen with a broad-based dural tail (green arrow) and non-aggressive bony remodeling (yellow arrow).

fields.Text: A jugular foramen lesion with a broad dural tail favors {{c1::meningioma}} over glomus jugulare or schwannoma.
fields.Extra: Q27. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. The images provided show a mass (red arrow) within the jugular foramen with a broad-based dural tail (green arrow) and non-aggressive bony remodeling (yellow arrow).

fields.Text:
Alcohol use disorder after rapid fluid resuscitation with pontine signal abnormality. Earliest imaging finding?

{{c1::Restricted diffusion}}
fields.Extra: Q28. Incorrect; difficulty: not listed. Vital Concept: Central pontine myelinolysis is earliest visualized on diffusion-weighted imaging (DWI), which shows hyperintense lesions before FLAIR, gadolinium enhancement, or T2-weighted changes appear.

fields.Text: Osmotic demyelination syndrome is earliest seen as {{c1::restricted diffusion}} before FLAIR or T2 changes.
fields.Extra: Q28. Incorrect; difficulty: not listed. Vital Concept: Central pontine myelinolysis is earliest visualized on diffusion-weighted imaging (DWI), which shows hyperintense lesions before FLAIR, gadolinium enhancement, or T2-weighted changes appear.

fields.Text: The earliest MRI finding in osmotic demyelination is {{c1::restricted diffusion}}, before enhancement or T2/FLAIR abnormality.
fields.Extra: Q28. Incorrect; difficulty: not listed. Vital Concept: Central pontine myelinolysis is earliest visualized on diffusion-weighted imaging (DWI), which shows hyperintense lesions before FLAIR, gadolinium enhancement, or T2-weighted changes appear.

fields.Text:
Gallbladder ultrasound with echogenic focus and complete posterior shadowing. Key diagnostic feature?

{{c1::Clean posterior acoustic shadowing}}
fields.Extra: Q29. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Clean posterior acoustic shadowing is essential for gallstone diagnosis on ultrasound—without it, the diagnosis of cholelithiasis cannot be confidently made, regardless of how echogenic a structure appears.

fields.Text: Clean posterior acoustic shadowing is the key ultrasound feature confirming {{c1::cholelithiasis}}.
fields.Extra: Q29. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Clean posterior acoustic shadowing is essential for gallstone diagnosis on ultrasound—without it, the diagnosis of cholelithiasis cannot be confidently made, regardless of how echogenic a structure appears.

fields.Text:
Gallbladder wall gas with dirty shadowing and reverberation artifact. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Emphysematous cholecystitis}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q30. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Emphysematous cholecystitis on ultrasound shows hyperechoic reverberation artifacts with dirty acoustic shadowing from intramural gas, commonly occurring in diabetic patients and often misinterpreted as gallstones or porcelain gallbladder.

fields.Text: Emphysematous cholecystitis is associated with {{c1::diabetes mellitus}} and shows intramural gas with dirty shadowing.
fields.Extra: Q30. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Emphysematous cholecystitis on ultrasound shows hyperechoic reverberation artifacts with dirty acoustic shadowing from intramural gas, commonly occurring in diabetic patients and often misinterpreted as gallstones or porcelain gallbladder.

fields.Text:
Early pregnancy ultrasound with an echogenic chorionic bump in the gestational sac. Main risk?

{{c1::Miscarriage}}
fields.Extra: Q31. Correct; difficulty: moderate. This is a chorionic bump and is associated with an increased miscarriage rate, as much as twice the average rate.

fields.Text: A chorionic bump in an intrauterine gestational sac increases the risk of {{c1::miscarriage}}.
fields.Extra: Q31. Correct; difficulty: moderate. This is a chorionic bump and is associated with an increased miscarriage rate, as much as twice the average rate.

fields.Text:
Fetal ultrasound with bilateral choroid plexus cysts plus increased nuchal translucency and clenched hands. Associated aneuploidy?

{{c1::Trisomy 18}}
fields.Extra: Q32. Correct; difficulty: hard. The image shows bilateral choroid plexus cysts.

fields.Text: Bilateral/persistent choroid plexus cysts with IUGR, congenital heart defects, and overlapping fingers suggest {{c1::trisomy 18}}.
fields.Extra: Q32. Correct; difficulty: hard. The image shows bilateral choroid plexus cysts.

fields.Text:
Breast ultrasound with a complicated cyst. What aspirate should be sent for cytology?

{{c1::Bloody or blood-tinged fluid}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q33. Incorrect; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Aspirated breast cyst fluid should be sent for cytology only if bloody or if specifically requested by the patient, as cloudy yellow or green fluid has high false-positive rates and should not be routinely tested.

fields.Text: Aspirated breast cyst fluid should be sent for cytology only if it is {{c1::bloody, very dark/degraded blood, purulent, or specifically requested}}.
fields.Extra: Q33. Incorrect; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Aspirated breast cyst fluid should be sent for cytology only if bloody or if specifically requested by the patient, as cloudy yellow or green fluid has high false-positive rates and should not be routinely tested.

fields.Text: Routine cytology is not needed for cloudy breast cyst fluid; send cyst aspirate when it is {{c1::bloody or very dark}}.
fields.Extra: Q33. Incorrect; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Aspirated breast cyst fluid should be sent for cytology only if bloody or if specifically requested by the patient, as cloudy yellow or green fluid has high false-positive rates and should not be routinely tested.

fields.Text: Mo-99 breakthrough in Tc-99m samples is tested with a {{c1::dose calibrator}} using a lead shield/pig method.
fields.Extra: Q34. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Molybdenum-99 breakthrough from technetium-99m generators must not exceed 0.15 μCi Mo-99 per mCi Tc-99m for human administration, requiring routine quality control testing and NRC reporting if limits are exceeded.

fields.Text: Mo-99 contamination in Tc-99m eluate is a radionuclidic purity test performed with a {{c1::dose calibrator}}, not a GM counter.
fields.Extra: Q34. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Molybdenum-99 breakthrough from technetium-99m generators must not exceed 0.15 μCi Mo-99 per mCi Tc-99m for human administration, requiring routine quality control testing and NRC reporting if limits are exceeded.

fields.Text:
Ultrasound with a noncompressible dilated appendix. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Acute uncomplicated appendicitis}}
fields.Extra: Q35. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Acute appendicitis is an acute inflammation of the vermiform appendix. Ultrasound is the first line imaging modality in young patients due to its lack of ionizing radiation. Aperistaltic, noncompressible, dilated appendix (> 6mm) are the most common findings on ultrasound. Hyperechoic intraluminal appendicolith may accompany. Periappendicial fat stranding and fluid exist due to inflammation. In case of perforation, it is called complicated appendicitis.

fields.Text: Ultrasound findings of appendicitis include a {{c1::noncompressible appendix larger than 6 mm}} with possible hyperemia or appendicolith.
fields.Extra: Q35. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Acute appendicitis is an acute inflammation of the vermiform appendix. Ultrasound is the first line imaging modality in young patients due to its lack of ionizing radiation. Aperistaltic, noncompressible, dilated appendix (> 6mm) are the most common findings on ultrasound. Hyperechoic intraluminal appendicolith may accompany. Periappendicial fat stranding and fluid exist due to inflammation. In case of perforation, it is called complicated appendicitis.

fields.Text: A noncompressible, dilated appendix with an appendicolith supports {{c1::acute uncomplicated appendicitis}} when no abscess or perforation is shown.
fields.Extra: Q35. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Acute appendicitis is an acute inflammation of the vermiform appendix. Ultrasound is the first line imaging modality in young patients due to its lack of ionizing radiation. Aperistaltic, noncompressible, dilated appendix (> 6mm) are the most common findings on ultrasound. Hyperechoic intraluminal appendicolith may accompany. Periappendicial fat stranding and fluid exist due to inflammation. In case of perforation, it is called complicated appendicitis.

fields.Text: Radiographs are the recommended {{c1::initial imaging test}} for suspected osteomyelitis.
fields.Extra: Q36. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Plain film radiography is the initial imaging modality for suspected osteomyelitis but requires substantial bone destruction before changes are visible. Magnetic resonance imaging is the gold standard with high sensitivity, determining infection extent, and distinguishing soft tissue from bone involvement.

fields.Text: Respect for patient autonomy requires giving patients enough information to make {{c1::informed medical decisions}}.
fields.Extra: Q37. Correct; difficulty: easy. The radiologist is refusing to provide appropriate medical information to the patient, which prevents the patient from making an informed medical decision.

fields.Text:
Temporal bone image with arrowed rounded prominence from the basal turn of the cochlea. What structure is indicated?

{{c1::Cochlear promontory}}
fields.Extra: Q38. Correct; difficulty: easy. Knowledge of the complex anatomy of the inner ear is useful in arriving at a diagnosis or appropriate differential diagnosis, as many processes are anatomically confined.

fields.Text: The cochlear promontory is formed by the outward projection of the {{c1::first turn of the cochlea}}.
fields.Extra: Q38. Correct; difficulty: easy. Knowledge of the complex anatomy of the inner ear is useful in arriving at a diagnosis or appropriate differential diagnosis, as many processes are anatomically confined.

fields.Text:
CT with bilateral lower-lobe fat-attenuation consolidations. Most likely associated history?

{{c1::Constipation/mineral oil aspiration}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q39. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Lipoid pneumonia is a form of pneumonia associated with oily or lipid components within the pneumonitis component. This can result from aspiration of oily substances (exogenous lipoid pneumonia) or endogenous accumulation of lipid substances in the alveoli (endogenous lipoid pneumonia).

fields.Text: Lower-lobe fat-attenuation consolidation suggests {{c1::exogenous lipoid pneumonia}}, classically from mineral oil aspiration.
fields.Extra: Q39. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Lipoid pneumonia is a form of pneumonia associated with oily or lipid components within the pneumonitis component. This can result from aspiration of oily substances (exogenous lipoid pneumonia) or endogenous accumulation of lipid substances in the alveoli (endogenous lipoid pneumonia).

fields.Text:
Proptosis and headache with expanded frontal sinus lesion causing smooth bony remodeling. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Sinonasal mucocele}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q40. Correct; difficulty: easy. The non-contrast T1-weighted MR shows a hyperintense opacified expanded left frontal sinus (red arrow) with smooth bony remodeling of walls (blue arrows), which, along with the clinical symptoms, is suggestive of sinonasal mucocele.

fields.Text: A sinonasal mucocele causes {{c1::sinus expansion with smooth bony remodeling}} and may produce proptosis or diplopia.
fields.Extra: Q40. Correct; difficulty: easy. The non-contrast T1-weighted MR shows a hyperintense opacified expanded left frontal sinus (red arrow) with smooth bony remodeling of walls (blue arrows), which, along with the clinical symptoms, is suggestive of sinonasal mucocele.

fields.Text:
Brain MRI with medusa-head venous drainage pattern. What lesion is commonly associated?

{{c1::Cavernous malformation}}
fields.Extra: Q41. Correct; difficulty: moderate. The finding demonstrated represents a developmental venous anomaly (DVA)( blue arrows), which terminates in a medusa head configuration (green arrows).

fields.Text: Developmental venous anomalies are commonly associated with {{c1::cavernous malformations}}.
fields.Extra: Q41. Correct; difficulty: moderate. The finding demonstrated represents a developmental venous anomaly (DVA)( blue arrows), which terminates in a medusa head configuration (green arrows).

fields.Text:
19-month-old with large heterogeneous renal mass and claw sign. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Wilms tumor}}
fields.Extra: Q42. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: The combination of claw sign confirming renal origin, appropriate age demographics, and characteristic heterogeneous enhancement pattern creates the classic presentation of Wilms' tumor, the most common primary renal malignancy in children and the most frequent cause of renal masses in the pediatric population.

fields.Text: Wilms tumor is the most common pediatric renal malignancy and classically presents as a {{c1::large heterogeneous renal mass}} in early childhood.
fields.Extra: Q42. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: The combination of claw sign confirming renal origin, appropriate age demographics, and characteristic heterogeneous enhancement pattern creates the classic presentation of Wilms' tumor, the most common primary renal malignancy in children and the most frequent cause of renal masses in the pediatric population.

fields.Text:
Atrial fibrillation patient with smooth left atrial filling defect extending into a pulmonary vein. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Left atrial thrombus}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q43. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Another key differential is an atrial myxoma. Atrial myxomas often are low-attenuation masses which are frequently attached to the interatrial septum by a thin stalk. They are most commonly located in the left atrium. They are typically highly mobile within the atrium and may prolapse into the mitral valve, producing a ball-valve effect. Occasionally they may have a frond-like appearance. In these cases, they are more likely to result in distal embolic disease, such as stroke.

fields.Text: Atrial fibrillation predisposes to {{c1::left atrial thrombus}} from slow/turbulent atrial flow.
fields.Extra: Q43. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Another key differential is an atrial myxoma. Atrial myxomas often are low-attenuation masses which are frequently attached to the interatrial septum by a thin stalk. They are most commonly located in the left atrium. They are typically highly mobile within the atrium and may prolapse into the mitral valve, producing a ball-valve effect. Occasionally they may have a frond-like appearance. In these cases, they are more likely to result in distal embolic disease, such as stroke.

fields.Text:
CT findings of mesenteric venous thrombosis in a patient with postprandial pain. What is true about the etiology?

{{c1::It is an uncommon cause of mesenteric ischemia}}
fields.Extra: Q44. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Mesenteric venous thrombosis is an uncommon but important cause of bowel ischemia, representing a small fraction of acute abdominal surgical cases but requiring high clinical suspicion due to its nonspecific presentation and high mortality if missed.

fields.Text: Mesenteric venous thrombosis accounts for only {{c1::5% to 15%}} of acute mesenteric ischemia cases.
fields.Extra: Q44. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Mesenteric venous thrombosis is an uncommon but important cause of bowel ischemia, representing a small fraction of acute abdominal surgical cases but requiring high clinical suspicion due to its nonspecific presentation and high mortality if missed.

fields.Text: Mesenteric venous thrombosis is a {{c1::less common}} cause of mesenteric ischemia than arterial occlusion.
fields.Extra: Q44. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Mesenteric venous thrombosis is an uncommon but important cause of bowel ischemia, representing a small fraction of acute abdominal surgical cases but requiring high clinical suspicion due to its nonspecific presentation and high mortality if missed.

fields.Text: Screen-film radiography has greater spatial resolution than computed radiography because CR is limited by {{c1::laser spot size during plate scanning}}.
fields.Extra: Q45. Incorrect; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Film screen radiography records information through continuous analog processes without discrete sampling limitations, while CR systems are fundamentally constrained by digital sampling via laser spot size—creating an insurmountable physical barrier to fine spatial resolution that cannot be overcome regardless of phosphor technology improvements.

fields.Text: Compared with CR, screen-film radiography has {{c1::higher spatial resolution}} because it is not limited by digital laser sampling.
fields.Extra: Q45. Incorrect; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Film screen radiography records information through continuous analog processes without discrete sampling limitations, while CR systems are fundamentally constrained by digital sampling via laser spot size—creating an insurmountable physical barrier to fine spatial resolution that cannot be overcome regardless of phosphor technology improvements.

fields.Text: Increasing the number of ultrasound focal zones improves {{c1::lateral resolution}} over a larger depth range but reduces frame rate.
fields.Extra: Q46. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Increasing the number of focal zones improves lateral resolution over a greater depth range but reduces frame rate since multiple transmit pulses are required.

fields.Text: Truncus arteriosus is almost always associated with a {{c1::ventricular septal defect}}.
fields.Extra: Q47. Incorrect; difficulty: not listed. Vital Concept: Truncus arteriosus most commonly presents with a subarterial ventricular septal defect.

fields.Text: The congenital anomaly most commonly associated with truncus arteriosus is {{c1::ventricular septal defect}}.
fields.Extra: Q47. Incorrect; difficulty: not listed. Vital Concept: Truncus arteriosus most commonly presents with a subarterial ventricular septal defect.

fields.Text:
Pelvic MRI with broad shallow fundal indentation and normal external uterine contour. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Arcuate uterus}}
fields.Extra: Q48. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Arcuate uterus demonstrates internal fundal indentation less than 1 cm with an angle greater than 90°, and recent classifications group it with normal uterus rather than as a separate müllerian duct anomaly.

fields.Text: An arcuate uterus has a broad internal fundal indentation less than {{c1::1 cm}} with a normal external fundal contour.
fields.Extra: Q48. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Arcuate uterus demonstrates internal fundal indentation less than 1 cm with an angle greater than 90°, and recent classifications group it with normal uterus rather than as a separate müllerian duct anomaly.

fields.Text:
Fatty liver ultrasound with focal hypoechoic regions lacking mass effect. Most likely explanation?

{{c1::Focal fatty sparing from altered perfusion}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q49. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. The images demonstrate a diffusely hyperechoic background liver parenchyma (yellow arrows) compatible with diffuse steatosis, with hypoechoic foci (blue arrows).

fields.Text: Focal fatty sparing occurs because of {{c1::altered perfusion characteristics}} relative to the rest of the fatty liver.
fields.Extra: Q49. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. The images demonstrate a diffusely hyperechoic background liver parenchyma (yellow arrows) compatible with diffuse steatosis, with hypoechoic foci (blue arrows).

fields.Text: Focal fatty sparing is a pseudolesion from {{c1::regional perfusion differences}}, not a neoplasm or separate fat-containing mass.
fields.Extra: Q49. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. The images demonstrate a diffusely hyperechoic background liver parenchyma (yellow arrows) compatible with diffuse steatosis, with hypoechoic foci (blue arrows).

fields.Text:
Hypervascular carcinoid liver metastasis undergoing embolization. What prophylaxis prevents carcinoid crisis?

{{c1::Pre-procedure octreotide}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q50. Correct; difficulty: not listed. Vital Concept: Short-acting subcutaneous octreotide should be administered one hour before procedures in patients with neuroendocrine tumors. Additional intravenous octreotide doses may be required if carcinoid crisis occurs during the procedure. Continuous octreotide infusion may be necessary for severe symptoms.

fields.Text: Patients with neuroendocrine tumor metastases should receive {{c1::short-acting octreotide before embolization/procedures}} to prevent carcinoid crisis.
fields.Extra: Q50. Correct; difficulty: not listed. Vital Concept: Short-acting subcutaneous octreotide should be administered one hour before procedures in patients with neuroendocrine tumors. Additional intravenous octreotide doses may be required if carcinoid crisis occurs during the procedure. Continuous octreotide infusion may be necessary for severe symptoms.
